summary: Codex runtime direction supersedes further polishing of the failed shared Haystack backend; official CLI JSONL, immutable governed tools and execpolicy proved on a real complex HVAC turn.

Independent review rejected workspace-write, shared evidence, raw thread IDs, upload mismatch and substring-grounding. The adapter now uses fresh ephemeral sessions, elevated Windows Permission Profiles, a required official MCP server with three fixed tools, private DuckDB outside command access, structured exact-excerpt citations, sanitized failures and truthful fixed-synthetic UI. Fourteen runtime tests, fifty focused regressions, browser acceptance and Ruff pass; secure real-model acceptance remains blocked pending administrator approval for elevated sandbox setup.

The remaining independent-review findings were remediated with TDD: telemetry citations now require non-empty data_quality or cop_ranking results, Codex mode skips the legacy embedding/reranking stack, and run-codex performs an elevated allow/deny sandbox preflight before starting the Web service. Nineteen Codex-runtime tests and fifty-five focused runtime/direction/Web regressions pass; real-model acceptance remains blocked pending administrator approval for elevated sandbox setup.

Independent rereview found zero Critical and zero Important code findings after the secure startup, grounding and embedding-stack fixes. The branch remains blocked from merge or production claims because real elevated-sandbox and secure real-model acceptance require explicit administrator approval.

The Chairman authorized elevated Windows setup and UAC provisioning completed, but CodexSandboxOffline could still open application/root-repository files on the readable E: drive. The adapter now applies an explicit deny ACL to private DuckDB directories and requires an outside-source denial before writing a readiness marker, so Windows startup remains blocked. A no-model WSL2 Landlock probe with official Codex 0.144.5 passed workspace/private/root isolation and is the next backend candidate.

Post-UAC rereview found that schema-v1 two-probe markers could be reused after the application-source probe was added. The marker schema is now version 2, all older markers are rejected, and readiness requires workspace allow plus private-DuckDB and application-source denials. Earlier pre-UAC review conclusions are marked superseded.
